annotations.label: Square (definition)

anchor.snippet: a square is a regular quadrilateral

annotations.note: Mathlib has no geometric definition of a square or regular quadrilateral as a planar figure.

annotations.label: Equivalent characterizations of a square

anchor.snippet: Squares can be defined or characterized in many equivalent ways

annotations.note: No geometric square object exists in Mathlib, so its equivalent characterizations are not formalized.

annotations.label: Square is the unique equiangular-equi­external regular polygon

anchor.snippet: Squares are the only regular polygons whose internal angle

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize regular polygons or their internal angles in this sense.

annotations.label: Square as a special case sharing all shape properties

anchor.snippet: A square is a special case of a rhombus

annotations.note: Mathlib has no rhombus/rectangle/quadrilateral hierarchy to express the square as a special case.

annotations.label: All squares are similar

anchor.snippet: All squares are similar to each other

annotations.note: Geometric similarity of polygons such as squares is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Perimeter and diagonal length

anchor.snippet: A square whose four sides have length

annotations.note: The perimeter and diagonal (s√2) formulas for a square are not stated in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: A square whose four sides have length

annotations.label: Irrationality of √2

anchor.snippet: The square root of 2 , appearing in this formula, is irrational

annotations.note: `irrational_sqrt_two : Irrational (√2)` is proven directly in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: The square root of 2 , appearing in this formula, is irrational

annotations.label: Area of a square

anchor.snippet: A square's area is

annotations.note: There is no named result giving the area of a square as side squared in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square number

anchor.snippet: results in a square number

annotations.note: `IsSquare a := ∃ r, a = r * r` specializes on ℕ to exactly the square numbers.

annotations.label: 4×4 square is equable

anchor.snippet: a four by four square has an area equal to its perimeter

annotations.note: The equable-square example is not formalized, as squares have no area/perimeter notion in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Isoperimetric property of the square

anchor.snippet: a square is the quadrilateral of least perimeter enclosing a given area

annotations.note: No isoperimetric inequality for quadrilaterals or the square is present in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: a square is the quadrilateral of least perimeter enclosing a given area

annotations.label: Eight symmetries of a square

anchor.snippet: Eight rigid transformations of the plane take the square to itself

annotations.note: Mathlib's `DihedralGroup 4` is the abstract symmetry group but is not realized as rigid plane transformations fixing a geometric square.

annotations.label: Symmetries act transitively on vertices and edges

anchor.snippet: symmetries of a square and, more generally, a regular polygon act transitively on vertices and edges

annotations.note: The transitive action of polygon symmetries on geometric vertices and edges is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dihedral group of order eight

anchor.snippet: the eight symmetries of a square the mathematical structure of a group

annotations.note: `DihedralGroup 4` with `DihedralGroup.card : Fintype.card (DihedralGroup n) = 2 * n` gives the order-8 dihedral group.

annotations.label: Affine and projective images of a square

anchor.snippet: An affine transformation can take a square to any parallelogram

annotations.note: Mathlib has affine maps but no statement about affine/projective images of a square.

annotations.label: Wallpaper groups requiring a square unit cell

anchor.snippet: for three of these groups, p4, p4m, and p4g, it must be a square

annotations.note: Wallpaper groups and their unit cells are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inscribed circle of a square

anchor.snippet: The inscribed circle of a square is the largest circle that can fit inside that square

annotations.note: The incircle of a square is not formalized, as squares are not defined in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: The inscribed circle of a square is the largest circle that can fit inside that square

annotations.label: Circumscribed circle of a square

anchor.snippet: The circumscribed circle of a square passes through all four vertices

annotations.note: The circumcircle of a square is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: The circumscribed circle of a square passes through all four vertices

annotations.label: Tangency-point distance identity

anchor.snippet: If the inscribed circle of a square

annotations.note: This tangency-point distance identity for a square is not present in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: If the inscribed circle of a square

annotations.label: Sum of squared distances to vertices

anchor.snippet: is the distance from an arbitrary point in the plane to the

annotations.note: While Euclidean distance infrastructure exists, this specific sum-of-squared-distances identity for square vertices is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: is the distance from an arbitrary point in the plane to the

annotations.label: Centroid and vertex distance identity

anchor.snippet: are the distances from an arbitrary point in the plane to the centroid of the square

annotations.note: This centroid–vertex distance identity for a square is not stated in Mathlib.

anchors.snippet: are the distances from an arbitrary point in the plane to the centroid of the square

annotations.label: Unit square

anchor.snippet: A unit square is a square of side length one

annotations.note: Mathlib has `unitInterval` but no dedicated unit-square object.

annotations.label: Axis-parallel square coordinates

anchor.snippet: An axis-parallel square with its center at the point

annotations.note: Coordinate descriptions of an axis-parallel square are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diagonal square coordinates

anchor.snippet: A diagonal square with its center at the point

annotations.note: Coordinate equations for a diagonally-oriented square are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square from repeated multiplication by i

anchor.snippet: these numbers will form the vertices of a square centered at the origin

annotations.note: The claim that powers of i give square vertices is not formalized geometrically in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gaussian integers form a square lattice

anchor.snippet: The Gaussian integers , complex numbers with integer real and imaginary parts, form a square lattice

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `GaussianInt` (ℤ[i]) as a ring, but not its embedding in ℂ as a square lattice.

annotations.label: Squares are constructible polygons

anchor.snippet: squares are constructible polygons

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Constructible` refers to constructible sets in algebraic geometry, not compass-and-straightedge constructible polygons.

annotations.label: Constructibility of regular n-gons

anchor.snippet: is constructible exactly when the odd prime factors of

annotations.note: The Gauss–Wantzel theorem on constructible regular n-gons is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inscribed and circumscribed square constructions

anchor.snippet: Elements IV.6–7 also give constructions for a square inscribed in a circle

annotations.note: Euclidean compass-and-straightedge constructions of inscribed/circumscribed squares are not formalized.

annotations.label: Schläfli symbol of a square

anchor.snippet: The Schläfli symbol of a square is

annotations.note: Schläfli symbols of polytopes are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Truncated square is an octagon

anchor.snippet: A truncated square is an octagon

annotations.note: Polygon truncation operations are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square in the hypercube and cross-polytope families

anchor.snippet: The square belongs to a family of regular polytopes that includes the cube

annotations.note: The hypercube and cross-polytope regular families are not formalized as polytopes in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Sierpiński carpet

anchor.snippet: The Sierpiński carpet is a square fractal

annotations.note: The Sierpiński carpet fractal is not formalized in Mathlib (only the unrelated Sierpiński topology).

annotations.label: Space-filling curves cover a square

anchor.snippet: cover a square as the continuous image of a line segment

annotations.note: Space-filling (Hilbert/Peano) curves are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Finsler–Hadwiger theorem

anchor.snippet: The Finsler–Hadwiger theorem states that for two squares

annotations.note: The Finsler–Hadwiger theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Van Aubel's theorem

anchor.snippet: the centers of four squares constructed on the sides of a quadrilateral form a midsquare quadrilateral

annotations.note: Van Aubel's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Monsky's theorem

anchor.snippet: A square cannot be dissected into an odd number of equal-area triangles

annotations.note: Monsky's theorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cross's / Vecten's theorem

anchor.snippet: Cross's theorem or Vecten's theorem states that

annotations.note: Cross's/Vecten's theorem on squares erected on a triangle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Box-counting (Minkowski–Bouligand) dimension

anchor.snippet: Minkowski–Bouligand dimension or box-counting dimension involves squares to determine the fractal dimension

annotations.note: The box-counting/Minkowski–Bouligand dimension is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square trisection

anchor.snippet: A square trisection is a dissection problem of cutting a square

annotations.note: Square trisection dissection problems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tarski's circle-squaring problem

anchor.snippet: Tarski's circle-squaring problem challenges the equidecomposability between a square and a circle

annotations.note: Tarski's circle-squaring and equidecomposability of a disk with a square are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inscribed square in a curve

anchor.snippet: A square is inscribed in a curve when all four vertices of the square lie on the curve

annotations.note: The notion of a square inscribed in a curve is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inscribed square problem

anchor.snippet: The unsolved inscribed square problem asks whether every simple closed curve has an inscribed square

annotations.note: The (open) inscribed square problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Equilateral triangle inscribable in convex curves

anchor.snippet: The only other regular polygon that can always be inscribed in every closed convex curve is the equilateral triangle

annotations.note: Inscribability of equilateral triangles in convex curves is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inscribed squares in triangles

anchor.snippet: For an inscribed square in a triangle , at least one side of the square lies on a side of the triangle

annotations.note: Results on squares inscribed in triangles are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Inscribed square covers at most half the triangle

anchor.snippet: A square inscribed in a triangle can cover at most half the triangle's area

annotations.note: The area bound for a square inscribed in a triangle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quadrature (squaring)

anchor.snippet: a process called quadrature or squaring

annotations.note: Quadrature in the classical area-construction sense is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem (areas of squares)

anchor.snippet: squares constructed on the two sides of a right triangle have equal total area to a square constructed on the hypotenuse

annotations.note: Mathlib proves the Pythagorean theorem in inner-product/angle form, generalizing the right-triangle square-areas statement.

annotations.label: Impossibility of squaring the circle

anchor.snippet: the task was proven to be impossible as a consequence of the Lindemann–Weierstrass theorem

annotations.note: Neither the transcendence of π nor the Lindemann–Weierstrass theorem is in Mathlib, so squaring-the-circle impossibility is not formalized.

annotations.label: Square tiling is one of three regular tilings

anchor.snippet: is one of three regular tilings of the plane

annotations.note: Regular tilings of the plane are not classified or formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vertices of a square tiling form a square lattice

anchor.snippet: The vertices of a square tiling form a square lattice

annotations.note: The square tiling and its vertex lattice are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Square packing problem

anchor.snippet: Square packing problems seek the smallest square or circle into which a given number of unit squares can fit

annotations.note: Square packing problems are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: NP-completeness of square packing

anchor.snippet: testing whether a given number of unit squares can fit into an orthogonally convex rectilinear polygon

annotations.note: NP-completeness of square packing is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Perfect squared square

anchor.snippet: A subdivision with distinct smaller squares is called a perfect squared square

annotations.note: Perfect squared squares are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Plane tiled by one square of each integer side

anchor.snippet: The entire plane can be tiled by squares, with exactly one square of each integer side length

annotations.note: This integer-side square tiling of the plane is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Clifford torus

anchor.snippet: the Clifford torus , the four-dimensional Cartesian product of two congruent circles

annotations.note: The Clifford torus is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Regular skew apeirohedron

anchor.snippet: a regular skew apeirohedron in three dimensions, has six squares meeting at each vertex

annotations.note: Regular skew apeirohedra are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Paper bag problem

anchor.snippet: The paper bag problem seeks the maximum volume

annotations.note: The paper bag problem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Biscornu

anchor.snippet: a pincushion shape called a biscornu

annotations.note: The biscornu shape is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Polyominoids

anchor.snippet: The surfaces tiled with finitely many squares of the three-dimensional integer lattice are called polyominoids

annotations.note: Polyominoids are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Counting squares in a 3×3 grid

anchor.snippet: a square grid of nine squares has 14 squares

annotations.note: Counting subsquares in a grid is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Counting squares from a grid of points

anchor.snippet: the number of squares formed by a grid of

annotations.note: The formula counting squares in a grid of points is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dividing a square into similar rectangles

anchor.snippet: A square can be divided into two similar rectangles only in one way

annotations.note: Dividing a square into similar rectangles is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Magic square

anchor.snippet: A magic square is a square array of numbers

annotations.note: Magic squares are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No right-angled quadrilaterals in curved space

anchor.snippet: quadrilaterals with four right angles do not exist

annotations.note: The nonexistence of right-angled quadrilaterals in curved geometry is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Angular excess (spherical square)

anchor.snippet: has angles whose sum exceeds 360° by an amount called the angular excess

annotations.note: Spherical angular excess is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spherical cube face

anchor.snippet: the face of a spherical cube with four 120° angles

annotations.note: Spherical cube faces are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Octant as a regular spherical triangle

anchor.snippet: An octant of a sphere is a regular spherical triangle

annotations.note: Spherical triangles such as the octant are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spherical lune as a regular digon

anchor.snippet: A spherical lune is a regular digon

annotations.note: Spherical lunes and digons are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Angular defect (hyperbolic square)

anchor.snippet: has angles whose sum falls short of 360° by an amount called the angular defect

annotations.note: Hyperbolic angular defect is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ideal square

anchor.snippet: an ideal square has four sides of infinite length

annotations.note: Ideal squares in hyperbolic geometry are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Squares as circles in taxicab geometry

anchor.snippet: Squares tilted at 45° to the coordinate axes are the circles in taxicab geometry

annotations.note: The identification of L¹ balls with tilted squares is not stated geometrically in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Squares as circles in Chebyshev distance

anchor.snippet: axis-parallel squares are the circles for the

annotations.note: The identification of L∞ balls with axis-parallel squares is not stated geometrically in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Squircle

anchor.snippet: a square and circle can become an intermediate shape known as squircle

annotations.note: The squircle is not formalized in Mathlib.
